statement.en: The First Challenge on Remote Sensing Infrared Image Super-Resolution at NTIRE 2026: Benchmark Results and Method Overview
statement.summary: This paper presents the NTIRE 2026 Remote Sensing Infrared Image Super-Resolution (x4) Challenge, one of the associated challenges of NTIRE 2026. The challenge aims to recover high-resolution (HR) infrared images from low-resolution (LR) inputs generated through bicubic downsampling with a x4 scaling factor. The objective is to develop effective models or solutions that achieve state-of-the-art performance for infrared image SR in remote sensing scenarios. To reflect the characteristics of infrared data and practical application needs, the challenge adopts a single-track setting. A total of 115 participants registered for the competition, with 13 teams submitting valid entries. This report summarizes the challenge design, dataset, evaluation protocol, main results, and the representative m

proposer: Kai Liu, Haoyang Yue, Zeli Lin et al.

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: Paper describes a 2026 challenge competition with preliminary results. Status is post-event but represents a specific benchmark snapshot rather than a validated persistent claim. The technical methods described are implementable and reproducible, but the paper itself appears incomplete (abstract cuts off mid-sentence). Challenge outcomes are empirically observable but results remain in post-competition validation phase.

ramanujanFormula.interpretation: 赤外線の潜在的な構造は、解像度の欠落の逆像に隠された無限のスケール層で眠っている。

visualGlyph.interpretation: 低解像度の格子から高解像度への螺旋的な上昇は、赤外光の見えない領域を徐々に顕現させる。
